description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by three prominent metallic hoops. The wood has a rich, dark brown color with visible grain patterns, giving it a rustic and weathered appearance. The metallic hoops are a dull silver-gray, tightly encircling the barrel at the top, middle, and bottom, providing structural reinforcement. The top of the barrel is closed, with the wood planks forming a flat, circular surface, and there are no visible openings, handles, or spouts. The surface texture appears rough and matte, typical of aged wood, while the metal bands have a slightly smoother finish. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage areas, or near taverns and markets.
description: A rustic wooden barrel with three silver metal hoops, closed at the top and featuring a weathered wood texture. The barrel has a classic bulging shape and no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full bulging profile, three metal hoops, and the wood grain detail.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view with similar features, highlighting the curvature and the seamless wood staves.
description_view_2: Another side view, nearly identical to the others, emphasizing the uniformity of the wood and metal bands.
description_view_3: Back view, visually indistinguishable from the front, with the same wood and metal features.
